singapore.title: Lower age limit, lower fees but Community Care Apartments unlikely to see surge in demand: Analysts
singapore.summary: The changes widen the pool of eligible buyers and may nudge people to plan ahead, but the bigger hurdle is a reluctance to leave familiar surroundings.

singapore.title: Singapore, Brunei reaffirm commitment to strengthening bilateral ties during SM Lee’s visit
singapore.summary: The visit, from July 14 to 17, was for the 80th birthday celebrations of the Sultan of Brunei.

singapore.title: Lawyer linked to billion-dollar money laundering case accused of forgery
singapore.summary: Julia Chan I-Fei was charged with conspiring to commit forgery in relation to the purchase of a residential property by the spouse of Su Yongcan, a suspect in the case who remains at large.

singapore.title: Schools embrace World Cup final fever with live screenings, later starts and jersey days
singapore.summary: Staying up to watch the World Cup final is a tradition for many football fans in Singapore. This year, some schools are making it a little easier.

singapore.summary: Scammers will likely exploit WhatsApp's new feature, which, despite its safeguards, is not foolproof.

singapore.summary: Preliminary police investigations indicate that the suspect, acting out of resentment, allegedly assaulted the victim by strangling her for about 15 minutes.

singapore.title: Dear You movie sparks interest in China heritage tours, say travel agencies
singapore.summary: Since the movie began screening in Singapore, some tour agencies have seen enquiries increase by at least 15 per cent to Chaoshan, where the box office hit was filmed.

us.title: White House revives rule to deny green cards to migrants who use safety net programs
us.summary: The Trump administration is reviving a policy that would allow immigration officers to deny green cards to applicants who use public benefits like Medicaid, housing assistance or food stamps. The policy governs how officials can apply the “public charge” test, which is used to determine whether applicants are likely to rely on government support. …

us.summary: President Trump’s social media platform announced plans Thursday to sell Wall Street firms early access to its “most market-moving” posts. Trump Media & Technology Group, the parent company of Truth Social, said it would launch “Truth API,” a direct data feed that would give investment firms “real-time access to posts from the highest-ranking Truth Social…

us.title: Election handicapper shifts California House seat to Dems, Maine seat to GOP
us.summary: A leading election handicapper shifted its outcome projections for two U.S. House races in Maine and California on Thursday, as the Democratic and Republican parties wrestle to secure control of the lower chamber of Congress this midterm cycle. Larry Sabato’s Crystal Ball at the University of Virginia Center for Politics changed its prediction for California’s…

us.title: Railroad, airline labor mediator fired by Trump drops legal fight
us.summary: The Supreme Court’s blockbuster decision expanding President Trump’s firing power has reached another agency: one that works out labor disputes in the airline and railroad industries. Deirdre Hamilton, a Democratic appointee to the National Mediation Board who had been contesting her firing, has now dismissed her legal challenge. A judge on Thursday ordered the case…

us.title: White House says ICE traffic stops will continue after deadly shootings
us.summary: The comments came following widespread reports earlier in the week that those stops would be put on pause after two immigrants were fatally shot by ICE agents in early July.

us.title: Flood sirens blare in southcentral Texas as rivers reach perilous heights
us.summary: Flood sirens blared early Thursday in Comfort, Texas, as the Guadalupe River rose more than 30 feet in three hours, the U.S. Geological Survey said. The storm could bring even more rainfall than last year's deadly Fourth of July floods.

asia.title: US to focus counterterrorism efforts on left-wing groups, Rubio says
asia.summary: US Secretary of State Marco ⁠Rubio told officials from more than 60 countries on Thursday that the United States would seek to refocus international counterterrorism efforts on what he called “far-left terror”, arguing that left-wing violence had long been overlooked. The conference in Washington has sparked Democratic concerns that the Trump administration is politicising counterterrorism efforts and diverting resources from other extremist threats. asia.title: Govt aims to finish EU FTA talks this year
asia.summary: Thailand aims to conclude free trade agreement (FTA) negotiations with the European Union (EU) by the end of this year, although differences over agricultural products and raw materials remain unresolved.

asia.title: Anutin, Xi to meet in Shanghai
asia.summary: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul will meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in Shanghai on Friday during his visit to China, with Thai-Cambodian border security expected to be high on the agenda.

asia.title: Power plan for data centres readied
asia.summary: Thailand's energy authorities are intensifying efforts to finalise considerations for the country's pilot direct power purchase agreement (PPA) scheme, which is expected to support the rapid growth of data centres and cloud service businesses.

asia.title: 1 dead, nearly 100 injured in crush at India chariot festival
asia.summary: A sudden crowd surge at a popular Hindu festival in eastern India’s Odisha state on Thursday left at least one person dead and many sent to hospital, a news agency reported. The incident occurred as tens of thousands of people gathered in the coastal town of Puri for the annual Rath Yatra chariot festival, according to the Press Trust of India. The festival is considered one of the world’s oldest and largest religious processions. The centuries-old festival involves the idols of Hindu deities...

asia.title: DAP committed to Anwar’s Pakatan Harapan despite electoral setbacks, says party leader Anthony Loke
asia.summary: Malaysia's Democratic Action Party chief Anthony Loke tells CNA why he remains a believer in coalition politics – and how Pakatan Harapan is hopeful of winning the Aug 1 Negeri Sembilan state election.

asia.title: Commentary: India’s long-held strategy is paying off in the Indo-Pacific
asia.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s diplomatic blitz is the latest sign of middle powers drawing closer together. It has not gone unnoticed by Washington, says former foreign correspondent Nirmal Ghosh.

asia.title: ONEAM moves to sell disputed hotel
asia.summary: One Asset Management (ONEAM), the manager of Grand Royal Orchid Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust (GROREIT), has begun legal procedures after Royal Orchid Hotel (Thailand) (ROH) failed to repurchase the 4.87-billion-baht Royal Orchid Sheraton Riverside by the deadline, resulting in a default under the five-year buyback agreement.

briefing: Global markets are watching the US military's boarding of a sanctioned oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman, while the White House has revived a rule to deny green cards to migrants who use safety net programs, sparking concerns over immigration policies. In Singapore, SMRT Buses has won a contract to operate 26 bus services in Eunos and Serangoon from June 2027, taking over from SBS Transit, and the government is exploring ways to boost demand for Community Care Apartments. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones Index and other major indices are expected to react to these developments, with investors also keeping an eye on President Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, which plans to sell early access to its posts to Wall Street firms.

digests.singapore: The local news in Singapore is currently focused on a range of topics, including housing and transportation, with analysts predicting that recent changes to Community Care Apartments are unlikely to significantly boost demand due to reluctance from potential buyers to leave familiar surroundings. Meanwhile, international relations and crime are also making headlines, with Singapore reaffirming its commitment to strengthening ties with Brunei and a lawyer being charged with forgery in relation to a billion-dollar money laundering case. Additionally, other stories are highlighting everyday life in Singapore, such as schools embracing World Cup final fever and the potential impact of a new movie on tourism interest in China heritage tours.
digests.us: The US military has boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman, while the White House has revived a rule to deny green cards to migrants who use public benefits, and President Trump's social media platform is set to sell early access to its posts to Wall Street firms. Election projections have also shifted in key House races in California and Maine, with the Democratic and Republican parties vying for control of the lower chamber. Additionally, the White House has confirmed that ICE traffic stops will continue despite recent deadly shootings, and severe flooding is affecting southcentral Texas, prompting flood warnings and evacuations.
digests.asia: The US is shifting its counterterrorism focus to left-wing groups, sparking concerns of politicisation, while Thailand is pursuing economic agreements with the EU and strengthening ties with China. Regional leaders are also navigating domestic issues, such as Malaysia's Democratic Action Party reaffirming its commitment to the Pakatan Harapan coalition despite electoral setbacks. Meanwhile, India's diplomatic efforts in the Indo-Pacific are gaining traction, and the country is dealing with the aftermath of a deadly crowd surge at a Hindu festival in Odisha state.
digests.others: The spread of Canadian wildfire smoke across the US has prompted air quality alerts in over 20 states, while internationally, the US has launched new strikes in Iran, aiming to degrade its military capabilities. In other news, wealthy Gulf states are investing heavily in African countries, driven by diverse objectives, and the US is facing pressure to join the International Criminal Court, with Ilhan Omar renewing her push for membership. Meanwhile, various scandals and controversies are unfolding, including the sentencing of Italy's former motorway chief and accusations against a White House staffer for making bets on Trump speeches.
digests.tech: The recent headlines highlight issues with modern software development, including the breakage of Android Auto due to an over-the-air update and a vulnerability in FortiSandbox, demonstrating the risks of rapid development and deployment. Meanwhile, advancements in AI are being integrated into various applications, such as Fortnite's introduction of AI-powered personas and the development of ReasonGate, an explainable gate that blocks LLM prompt injection. These developments underscore the complex interplay between technological innovation, software reliability, and user experience.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the challenges posed by bureaucratic barriers to social activities in America, demographic problems in China exacerbated by antagonism between the sexes, and the shifting dynamics of power in Asia, where middle powers are drawing closer due to America's unreliability. The publication also examines the business landscape, noting the successes of SK Hynix in the memory chip industry and the importance of brand positioning in consumer markets. Additionally, it discusses the geopolitical implications of emerging technologies, such as sovereign AI, and the limitations of achieving true independence from major world powers.
